signals.title: U.S. Department of Education Finalizes Landmark Rule to Lower College Costs and Simplify Student Loan Repayment
signals.summary: Official rule finalized: starting July 1, 2026, new borrowers get only two repayment options — Tiered Standard Repayment Plan and the income-driven Repayment Assistance Plan (RAP). PAYE/ICR sunset by 2028, IBR stays only for pre-July-2026 loans. New borrowing caps: grad $20,500/yr ($100k lifetime), professional $50,000/yr ($200k lifetime), Parent PLUS $20,000/yr ($65k lifetime).
signals.relevance: Direct, structural change to how every new federal borrower repays loans starting this summer.
signals.reliability: Primary source, official government press release.

signals.title: Guide: Student loan options change July 1. What you need to know
signals.summary: 7.5 million SAVE-plan borrowers will have only 90 days to transition once SAVE shuts down.
signals.relevance: Hard deadline and large affected population.
signals.reliability: High — national public radio explainer citing federal data.

signals.title: Student Loan Repayments To Change in July 2026: What Borrowers Need To Know
signals.summary: Covers the RAP/Tiered Standard overhaul with borrower-facing framing.
signals.relevance: Mainstream consumer-facing coverage.

signals.title: New RAP student loan calculator shows what you'd owe monthly under the GOP proposal
signals.summary: Sustained borrower confusion about how RAP payments will be calculated.
signals.relevance: Real-time borrower engagement and confusion.
signals.reliability: Niche finance media account, widely followed.

signals.title: A Young Woman Recently Found Out She Had A 17% Interest Rate On Her Student Loans, And Her Tearful Reaction Is Going Viral
signals.summary: Secondary reporting on a viral TikTok of a borrower reacting to a 17% loan interest rate.
signals.relevance: TikTok-native emotional reaction format.
signals.reliability: Tabloid outlet summarizing a TikTok video; treat as color.

signals.title: Rap Student Loan Payments — TikTok discover page
signals.summary: Active TikTok discover topic aggregating creator videos on RAP loan payments.
signals.relevance: Confirms platform-native volume.
signals.reliability: Aggregated discover page; trend-volume color only.

signals.title: Repayment Assistance Plan (RAP) allows eligible Canadian students to defer loan payments
signals.summary: Canadian provincial student-loan account promoting its own RAP-style deferment plan.
signals.relevance: Canada-side comparison point on the same naming/topic.

signals.title: Emory will be tuition-free for students whose families earn $200,000 or less
signals.summary: Emory Advantage Plus makes Emory tuition-free starting fall 2026 for families earning $200,000 or less, part of a wider wave of free-tuition expansions (Yale, UNT, Bridgewater State).
signals.relevance: Concrete, comparable dollar thresholds across named schools.

signals.title: These Colleges Offer Free Tuition
signals.summary: Running list of colleges offering income-based free tuition for 2026: Yale, UNT, Bridgewater State.
signals.relevance: Comparison data for 'which schools are free for your income bracket'.

signals.title: Here are the college majors that new grads regret the most, according to a recent survey
signals.summary: ZipRecruiter survey: 20.5% regret their major overall; political science tops at 46.3%, communications 39.2%.
signals.relevance: Hard ranked data by major.

signals.title: Class of 2026: A depressed hires rate is a major cause of labor market weakness for young college graduates
signals.summary: Only 19% of grads say it's a good time to find a quality job, down from 70%+ in 2022.
signals.relevance: Strong before/after statistic.

signals.title: Most Regretted College Majors 2026: Poli Sci Tops List
signals.summary: Reinforces the ZipRecruiter ranking; nursing cited as standout positive outlier (~$70k, near-full pre-grad employment).
signals.relevance: Adds the 'winner' side to balance the regret ranking.

signals.title: New Student Visas Dropped 35.6% Last Summer
signals.summary: New US student visas fell 35.6% summer-over-summer; from ~286,000 to ~186,160; Indian student visas fell over 60%.
signals.relevance: Hard percentage drop with country-level breakdown.

signals.title: U.S. Sees 20% Drop in New International Students
signals.summary: Foreign enrollment fell 20% for spring 2026 vs. prior spring, per NAFSA-linked reporting.
signals.relevance: National enrollment-level confirmation.
signals.reliability: Established national news magazine citing NAFSA data.

signals.title: US to end "Duration of Status" for F, J, and I visas and limit the time international students can study in the US
signals.summary: From September 2026, most international students must finish their program within 4 years unless given a USCIS extension.
signals.relevance: Direct, decision-relevant rule change.

signals.title: US diplomats officially directed to screen the social media and online presence of all student visa applicants
signals.summary: Visa applicants must list every social media handle from the past 5 years and set accounts to public for vetting.
signals.relevance: Concrete, personal, shareable policy detail.

signals.title: All F, M, or J visa applicants requested to set social media accounts to 'public' to facilitate vetting
signals.summary: Official US Embassy account confirming the social-media public-setting requirement directly to applicants.
signals.relevance: Primary-source confirmation of the policy detail.

signals.title: Student visas for Africa's best and brightest were canceled by the Trump administration, leaving empty seats and broken dreams
signals.summary: Highlights visa cancellations specifically affecting African students.
signals.relevance: Broadens the visa-crackdown story beyond India.

signals.source_name: The Spectrum (University at Buffalo student newspaper)
signals.title: Living together, falling apart: the reality of roommate conflict
signals.summary: RA notes roommate conflict peaks early in the year before boundaries are set.
signals.relevance: First-person student newspaper account of a near-universal experience.

signals.title: COLUMN: Netflix's 'Roommates' exacerbates the experience of roommate collision
signals.summary: Connects the April 2026 Netflix film 'Roommates' to real dorm tension and roommate-matching anxiety.
signals.relevance: Direct tie between pop-culture release and real student anxiety.

signals.title: Widespread AI misuse means higher ed must rethink assessment
signals.summary: Study across 20 public research universities: ~1/3 of students regularly used generative AI on assignments, 9% admitted cheating with it.
signals.relevance: Largest known study with specific cheating figure and STEM/non-STEM split.

signals.title: The largest study of AI use by undergrads is in, revealing disparities in access — and in cheating
signals.summary: Largest-scale undergrad AI-use study, highlighting unequal access to premium AI tools alongside cheating-rate findings.
signals.relevance: Adds an equity/access angle.

signals.title: College students are cheating. Professors are struggling to stop them.
signals.summary: Princeton dropped a 133-year ban on exam proctoring; Oberlin changed its honor code; some professors reintroduced blue-book exams.
signals.relevance: Concrete institutional reversals.

signals.title: To avoid accusations of AI cheating, college students are turning to AI
signals.summary: Students using AI 'humanizer' tools to beat unreliable detectors that disproportionately flag non-native English speakers.
signals.relevance: Adds the false-positive/fairness angle.

signals.title: 12% of college students won't participate in an internship
signals.summary: 8.2 million students wanted to intern but 4.6 million couldn't get a spot; paid interns get offers at 66.4% vs 43.7% for unpaid.
signals.relevance: Direct demand/supply gap and outcome gap.
signals.reliability: Trade publication citing named research forum.

signals.title: Internship Statistics U.S. 2026
signals.summary: ~60.8% of US internships paid vs 39.2% unpaid; Handshake postings fell 15% from Jan 2023 to Jan 2025 while applications rose.
signals.relevance: Supply/demand divergence framing.

signals.title: The rise of a college influencer, from side hustle to six-figure career
signals.summary: Profile of a college student who turned content creation into a six-figure side career.
signals.relevance: Named, specific success story.

signals.source_name: Instagram post (HOW TO BUILD A TIKTOK SIDE HUSTLE IN COLLEGE)
signals.title: HOW TO BUILD A TIKTOK SIDE HUSTLE IN COLLEGE
signals.summary: Student-creator how-to post on building a content-creation side hustle while in college.
signals.relevance: Direct social-platform evidence.

signals.title: #collegehustle — TikTok tag page
signals.summary: Active TikTok hashtag aggregating student-creator side-hustle videos.
signals.relevance: Volume confirmation.
signals.reliability: Aggregated tag page; trend-volume color only.

signals.title: Invisible on Campus: The Student Loneliness Crisis (Research Brief)
signals.summary: Survey of ~44,000 students: 57% felt lonely (45% sometimes, 12% always).
signals.relevance: Large, named sample size with a clean headline percentage.

signals.title: Social Media Linked to Student Loneliness
signals.summary: University of Cincinnati study of ~65,000 students: 16+ hrs/week social media use linked to higher odds of loneliness.
signals.relevance: Behavioral hook directly relevant to students.

signals.title: Gen Z college students are looking for scholarships on TikTok. There's a better way, experts say
signals.summary: Gen Z students now search TikTok first for scholarship/financial-aid advice; experts warn of misinformation risk.
signals.relevance: Platform-behavior angle on financial aid.

signals.title: Why many college students are forced to spend more on housing than tuition
signals.summary: Students at public four-year institutions pay an average of $12,770 in room and board vs $11,260 in tuition and fees — housing has overtaken tuition as the bigger cost.
signals.relevance: National-broadcast confirmation that housing now outpaces tuition as the top college cost.

signals.title: The fastest-growing college cost? It's not tuition
signals.summary: Off-campus housing for students at 4-year public schools averages about $10,781/year (~$1,080/month over a 10-month academic year), with housing costs rising faster than tuition nationally.
signals.relevance: Specialist higher-ed outlet with a specific dollar figure for off-campus housing.

signals.title: North America's off-campus housing platform serving over 245 campuses and 3.5 million students
signals.summary: Off-campus housing platform post referencing the ongoing student housing shortage across North American campuses.
signals.relevance: Platform-native confirmation of housing-shortage chatter.
signals.reliability: Industry account with a commercial interest; treat as color.

signals.title: Commentary on a $3,100 rent figure for student housing cited as a 'human rights violation'
signals.summary: Real-estate commentator reacting to a news piece on student rent costs, breaking down the per-roommate split (~$1,550) and attributing the price to a housing supply shortage.
signals.relevance: Shows real-time public debate over specific student rent figures.
signals.reliability: Individual commentator account; treat as color/debate evidence, not a primary data source.

signals.title: Survey: 3 in 10 Students Lose Significant Academic Credits Transferring Between Colleges
signals.summary: National survey finds roughly 3 in 10 transfer students lose significant academic credits in the transfer process, delaying graduation.
signals.relevance: Named survey data quantifying a common transfer-student pain point.

signals.title: Thousands of Texas Students Lose College Credits, Costing Time and Money
signals.summary: More than 19,000 Texas community college students didn't get credit for at least one course when transferring to a university in 2023-24; 16% of affected students reported giving up on higher ed altogether.
signals.relevance: Concrete state-level numbers and a serious downstream consequence (dropping out).
signals.reliability: Regional news outlet citing state education data.

signals.title: Transfer and Learning Mobility in 2026 and Beyond
signals.summary: Opinion column on persistent structural barriers to credit transfer and learning mobility entering 2026.
signals.relevance: Frames the credit-loss problem as an ongoing, unresolved structural issue, not a one-off.
signals.reliability: Trade publication opinion column by a transfer-policy specialist.

signals.source_name: The Cavalier Daily (University of Virginia student newspaper)
signals.title: Fraternity and sorority recruitment sees influx in registration
signals.summary: 1,100 undergraduate women registered to rush 13 sororities and 825 men registered to rush 25 fraternities at UVA for the 2026 cycle — IFC's largest sign-up since extending its registration deadline; ISC reached its largest-ever sorority membership (3,082).
signals.relevance: Concrete, named-school registration numbers showing a real surge in Greek-life interest.
signals.reliability: Primary campus newspaper reporting with specific named figures.

signals.title: RushTok — TikTok discover page
signals.summary: Active 'RushTok' discover topic aggregating recruitment outfit, video-submission, and sisterhood content from sororities across many campuses.
signals.relevance: Confirms sustained platform-native volume on the rush/recruitment trend.
signals.reliability: Aggregated discover page; trend-volume color only.

signals.title: i show you my introduction video I submitted (and what I would change about it) in the pre rush prep club! #sororityrecruitment #rushvideo #rushtok
signals.summary: Creator-native rush-prep advice video circulating under the #rushtok tag.
signals.relevance: Direct creator evidence of active student engagement with rush content.

signals.title: 10 Universities Where Most Freshmen Commute
signals.summary: Ranks universities by share of freshmen who commute rather than live on campus, underscoring that commuting is the norm rather than the exception at many schools.
signals.relevance: Named, school-by-school comparison data for a commuter-vs-dorm format.

signals.title: More students are going to college. Affordability and workforce training are factors
signals.summary: Community college enrollment rose roughly 3% in fall 2025, with affordability cited as a key driver — many of these students are commuters living off-campus or at home.
signals.relevance: Connects rising commuter/community-college enrollment to affordability pressures.

signals.title: You save money but pay with your mental health 😭 #college #university #commuterlife
signals.summary: Viral creator video on the tradeoffs of commuter life — saving money on housing but losing social/mental-health benefits of living on campus.
signals.relevance: Direct student-voice framing of the financial-vs-wellbeing tradeoff in commuting.
